backlog.title: QA Bugfix Round — stdout discipline, flag-parse safety, exclusivity & repo hygiene
backlog.description: Fix the 7 issues surfaced by the end-to-end creative-QA pass against PRD §6/§8/§16. Two Major (init writes check report to stdout; init --store with no value silently overwrites the config) and five Minor (tag+--path not rejected; init init not rejected; tilde not expanded in init paths; .gitignore drifts from §16; check/init reserved tags undocumented). All fixes preserve the load-bearing §6.4 stdout-discipline and atomic-resolution contracts and stay stdlib-only besides yaml.v3. Research validated against the built binary at HEAD 414758d; findings in plan/002_38acb6d28a6a/bugfix/001_ad9c7988274c/architecture/.

milestones.title: Major bugfixes — init stdout contract + non-destructive flag parsing
milestones.description: Restore the two Major contract violations. Issue 1: init must emit ONLY the configured store path on stdout (the check report moves to stderr) so STORE="$(skilldozer init --store /path)" yields a clean single line. Issue 2: init --store (and --store=) presented with no value must fail fast (exit 2) and never overwrite an existing config via the auto-detect path.

tasks.title: Route init check-report to stderr (Issue 1)
tasks.description: In runInit the check-report render loop and summary use fmt.Fprintf(stdout, ...), so $(skilldozer init) captures the store path PLUS the full OK/WARN/ERROR report. Move the three report emitters to stderr; leave the store-path headline (fmt.Fprintln(stdout, dir)) on stdout. Do not touch exit codes (init stays 0; check findings never gate init). Do not refactor the mirrored if c.check block.

subtasks.title: Move init check-report emitters from stdout to stderr + strengthen the init stdout test
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See architecture/bug_fixes_validation.md §ISSUE 1 and architecture/system_context.md (runInit). The check-report block is main.go:1037-1053 inside runInit (main.go:988). The buggy emitters are: main.go:1046 `fmt.Fprintf(stdout, "%-5s %s (%s)\n", "OK", sr.Skill.RelTag, name)` (OK line); main.go:1050 `fmt.Fprintf(stdout, "%-5s %s (%s): %s\n", f.Level, sr.Skill.RelTag, name, f.Message)` (per-finding); main.go:1053 `fmt.Fprintf(stdout, "%d skills, %d errors, %d warnings\n", len(skills), rep.Errors, rep.Warnings)` (summary). The store-path headline `fmt.Fprintln(stdout, dir)` at main.go:1026 MUST STAY on stdout (PRD §6.1). The `(found via %s)` label at main.go:1029 is already on stderr.
2. INPUT: runInit receives (c config, stdout, stderr io.Writer). No new data needed; this is a writer-argument swap on existing emitters.
3. LOGIC: Implement PRD §6.1 stdout contract for init. Change the writer argument of the THREE check-report fmt.Fprintf calls (1046, 1050, 1053) from `stdout` to `stderr`. Leave main.go:1026 (store path) on stdout. Update the in-function comment (main.go:1037-1039 'Mirrors the if c.check branch render VERBATIM') to note init now diverges: init renders the check report to STDERR (per §6.1), while the standalone `check` subcommand keeps it on stdout. Do NOT extract a shared helper. No external services to mock.
4. OUTPUT: runInit prints exactly ONE line to stdout (the store dir) and the full check report + summary to stderr, for consumption by scripts/CI doing STORE="$(skilldozer init --store /path)". Exit code unchanged (0).
5. DOCS: [Mode A] Update the inline comment block at main.go:1037-1039 to reflect the stdout/stderr split. The README init section (README.md ~line 62-77) does not currently describe the check-report stream, so no per-subtask README edit is needed — the cross-cutting README sweep is the final Mode B task (P1.M3.T1).

tasks.title: Reject init --store with no value instead of silently overwriting the config (Issue 2)
tasks.description: When --store (or --store=) appears with no value, parseArgs silently no-ops; because the earlier init token set c.init=true with c.initStore=="", the invocation degrades to auto-detect init, which setupStore ALWAYS follows with config.Save — clobbering an existing valid store: value. Add a missing-value signal in parseArgs (both --store branches) and reject it in run() with exit 2 before init dispatches, without writing the config.

subtasks.title: Record a storeMissingValue signal in parseArgs for both --store no-value branches
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See architecture/bug_fixes_validation.md §ISSUE 2 and architecture/decisions.md §D2. parseArgs is main.go:153. The next-token `case "--store":` is main.go:257-266; the guard `if i+1 < len(args)` at main.go:263 silently skips when --store is the LAST token. The '='-form `--store=` is main.go:192-197 (inside the --flag=value splitter) and sets c.init=true; c.initStore="" for an empty value. The config struct is main.go:128-152.
2. INPUT: parseArgs(args []string) config. No new external data.
3. LOGIC: Add a new bool field `storeMissingValue` to the config struct (main.go:128) with a doc comment noting it signals '--store/--store= seen with no value' and that run() rejects it with exit 2. In the next-token `case "--store":` branch (main.go:257-266): when `i+1 >= len(args)` (no following token), set `c.storeMissingValue = true` (instead of doing nothing). In the '='-form branch (main.go:192-197): when val == "" after splitting, set `c.storeMissingValue = true` (you may still set c.init true there, but run() will reject before dispatch). Do NOT overload c.unknownFlag — that channel is 'first unknown dashed token'; --store is a KNOWN flag missing its value, a different error class. CRITICAL: bare `skilldozer init` with NO --store token legitimately has c.initStore=="" and MUST still prompt — only the explicit --store-with-no-value case sets the signal. No external services to mock.
4. OUTPUT: config.storeMissingValue is true iff --store/--store= appeared without a value, consumed by P1.M1.T2.S2 (run() guard). Expose the field on the config struct.
5. DOCS: [Mode A] Add the field doc comment in the config struct. No user-facing README surface change at this subtask — the exit-2-on-missing-value behavior is covered by the final Mode B README sweep (P1.M3.T1).

subtasks.title: Guard run() to reject missing --store value with exit 2 before init dispatch (config NOT written)
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See architecture/bug_fixes_validation.md §ISSUE 2. run() is main.go:408. The dispatch order is: help (416) → version (422) → unknownFlag (429) → exclusivityError (441) → init dispatch `if c.init { return runInit(c, stdout, stderr) }` at main.go:447. setupStore (main.go:948) ALWAYS calls configpkg.Save, which is why auto-detect init clobbers the config.
2. INPUT: config.storeMissingValue (bool) from P1.M1.T2.S1 (set true when --store/--store= had no value). Also c.init (bool) — the signal is only meaningful when --store was used to drive init.
3. LOGIC: Implement PRD §6 header 'Unknown flags ⇒ error + exit 2' and the delta-PRD 'init is non-destructive' constraint. In run(), AFTER the unknownFlag check (main.go:429-432) and BEFORE the exclusivity check (main.go:441) — or immediately before the init dispatch at main.go:447 — add: `if c.storeMissingValue { fmt.Fprintln(stderr, "skilldozer: --store requires a value"); return 2 }`. The message goes to STDERR and stdout stays EMPTY (§6.4 discipline). Because this returns before runInit is reached, setupStore/configpkg.Save is NEVER called — the existing config is preserved. Place the guard before exclusivityError OR before init dispatch; either is correct as long as it precedes runInit. Mirror the unknownFlag branch's style (one-line message to stderr, return 2). No external services to mock.
4. OUTPUT: `skilldozer init --store` (no value) and `--store=` (empty) exit 2 with a stderr message and an EMPTY stdout, and the config file is left untouched. Consumed by: the final acceptance suite and the README error-contract section.
5. DOCS: [Mode A] Update the run() precedence comment (main.go:413-415 region) to list the new '--store requires a value' guard in the precedence ladder. README error-contract section (README.md ~line 164-170) is swept by the final Mode B task (P1.M3.T1).

milestones.title: Minor bugfixes — exclusivity corners, init dedup, tilde expansion, gitignore hygiene, reserved-tag docs
milestones.description: Fix the five Minor issues. Issue 3: tag+--path must exit 2 like tag+--list. Issue 4: init init must exit 2 like init check. Issue 5: init/interactive/--store paths must expand a leading ~ to $HOME before filepath.Abs. Issue 6: .gitignore trimmed to the exact PRD §16 5-entry set. Issue 7: document that check/init are reserved tags (no code change).

tasks.title: Reject tag combined with --path (Issue 3)
tasks.description: exclusivityError omits c.path from the tags-conflict predicate even though --path IS in the listing-modes count set and the check+mode set, so `skilldozer NONEXISTENTTAG --path` silently runs --path and drops the tag. Add c.path to the predicate and update the user-facing message.

subtasks.title: Add c.path to the tags-conflict predicate in exclusivityError + test
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See architecture/bug_fixes_validation.md §ISSUE 3. exclusivityError is main.go:686. The buggy predicate is main.go:702: `if hasTags && (c.list || c.searchMode || c.all) { return true, "skilldozer: tags cannot be combined with --list/--search/--all" }`. The asymmetry: c.path is in the count set main.go:695 `{c.path, c.list, c.searchMode, c.all}` and the check+mode set main.go:708, but NOT in the tags predicate at 702. Sibling tests: TestRunExclusivityTagsAndList (main_test.go:1559), TestRunExclusivityCheckAndPath (main_test.go:1628) proves check+--path IS rejected.
2. INPUT: config c (has c.path bool, c.tags []string) from parseArgs.
3. LOGIC: Implement PRD §6.3 'Mixing <tag> with --list/--search/--all is an error (exit 2)' consistently for --path. Change main.go:702 to `if hasTags && (c.path || c.list || c.searchMode || c.all) { return true, "skilldozer: tags cannot be combined with --path/--list/--search/--all" }`. This is a one-line predicate change + message-string update. No exit-code or ordering change (still exit 2, still checked at the same point). No external services to mock.
4. OUTPUT: `skilldozer <tag> --path` (and `--path <tag>`) exit 2 with an empty stdout and the updated stderr message. Consumed by: the acceptance suite and the README error-contract section.
5. DOCS: [Mode A] Update the exclusivityError doc comment at main.go:686 to note --path is now part of the tags conflict. README error-contract (README.md ~line 168-170, which lists the mutually-exclusive modes) is swept by the final Mode B task (P1.M3.T1) to mention tags+--path.

tasks.title: Reject init init (Issue 4)
tasks.description: The parseArgs init case refuses to capture a following check/init token as the store, but a following init re-enters case "init" and re-sets c.init=true with no conflict, so `skilldozer init init` runs init (exit 0). Treat a second reserved init/check token after init as a conflict so exclusivityError rejects it (exit 2), matching init check.

subtasks.title: Capture a duplicate reserved init token as a conflict in parseArgs + test
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See architecture/bug_fixes_validation.md §ISSUE 4 and architecture/decisions.md §D3. parseArgs `case "init":` is main.go:268-284; c.init=true is set at main.go:277; the guard `next != "check" && next != "init"` at main.go:281 swallows a following reserved token instead of capturing it. exclusivityError's init branch is main.go:711-716 and rejects hasTags with "'init' cannot be combined with tag arguments". init check already works because check sets c.check (caught at main.go:712). Control test: TestRunExclusivityInitAndCheck (main_test.go:1679) → exit 2.
2. INPUT: parseArgs args; the token following init (args[i+1]).
3. LOGIC: Implement Option A (architecture/decisions.md §D3). In the `case "init":` branch (main.go:268-284), when the following token `next == "init"` (a duplicate init), append it to c.tags instead of swallowing it — e.g. change the guard so that a duplicate `init` (NOT a literal store dir, which must still use --store) is collected into c.tags. Then exclusivityError's init branch (main.go:713 `if hasTags`) fires and returns the existing "'init' cannot be combined with tag arguments" message → exit 2. Keep the existing behavior for `init <dir>` (positional store) and `init --store <dir>`. A literal store dir named init/check must still be passed via --store (already documented in the case comment). No new config field needed. No external services to mock.
4. OUTPUT: `skilldozer init init` exits 2 with an empty stdout and the 'init' cannot be combined message, and the config is NOT written (exclusivityError fires before init dispatch). Consistent with init check. Consumed by: the acceptance suite.
5. DOCS: [Mode A] Update the `case "init":` comment (main.go:269-280) to note a duplicate init token is now treated as a conflict. No README surface change — covered by the final Mode B sweep (P1.M3.T1).

tasks.title: Expand tilde (~) in init/interactive/--store paths (Issue 5)
tasks.description: resolveStore absolutizes the chosen store with filepath.Abs, which does NOT expand ~, so `~/myskills` becomes <cwd>/~/myskills and a dir literally named ~ is created. Add an expandHome helper (stdlib os.UserHomeDir) and call it in resolveStore before filepath.Abs. Affects interactive typed paths, init <dir>, and --store <dir>.

subtasks.title: Add the expandHome helper (stdlib os.UserHomeDir) + table-driven tests
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See architecture/go_tilde_expansion.md (full algorithm + edge-case table) and architecture/decisions.md §D4. There is NO stdlib tilde function (os.Expand/os.ExpandEnv handle only $VAR). Canonical pattern: os.UserHomeDir() + strings.HasPrefix(p, "~/") + bare "~" special case. os.UserHomeDir is already used in internal/config/config.go:154 (DefaultStore) — reuse for consistency. filepath.Abs does NOT expand ~, so expansion MUST run before it.
2. INPUT: a path string p (the user-typed/init/--store value).
3. LOGIC: Add `func expandHome(p string) string` in main.go, adjacent to resolveStore (main.go:865). Behavior: p=="~" → os.UserHomeDir() if no error else p unchanged; strings.HasPrefix(p, "~/") → filepath.Join(home, p[2:]) if no error else p unchanged; everything else (including ~user, ~foo, empty, relative, absolute) → returned UNCHANGED. On os.UserHomeDir error ($HOME unset), return p unchanged (fail safe — docs say caller may ignore the error). main.go already imports os, strings, path/filepath — no new imports. Do NOT widen the pure chooseStore signature (main.go:822 returns its choice verbatim by design). Mock: none (os.UserHomeDir is env-driven; tests set HOME via t.Setenv).
4. OUTPUT: expandHome(p) string, a pure-ish helper (env-dependent only via HOME) consumed by P1.M2.T3.S2. Expose it as a package-level func so it is directly unit-testable.
5. DOCS: [Mode A] Add the expandHome doc comment (per architecture/go_tilde_expansion.md snippet) covering the ~ / ~/ / ~user / no-HOME cases and the before-filepath.Abs ordering note. No README surface change at this subtask — the init tilde behavior is covered by the final Mode B sweep (P1.M3.T1).

subtasks.title: Wire expandHome into resolveStore before filepath.Abs + integration test
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See architecture/bug_fixes_validation.md §ISSUE 5. resolveStore is main.go:865; the buggy absolutize is main.go:886 `abs, err := filepath.Abs(store)`. store is the verbatim return of chooseStore (haveStore from c.initStore, or the typed prompt path). Call site runInit → resolveStore(c.initStore) at main.go:990-994.
2. INPUT: expandHome(p) string from P1.M2.T3.S1.
3. LOGIC: In resolveStore (main.go:865), between the chooseStore call and the filepath.Abs call (main.go:886), expand ~ first: `store = expandHome(store)` then `abs, err := filepath.Abs(store)`. This makes init <dir>, --store <dir>, --store=<dir>, AND the interactive typed path all expand ~ to $HOME. Idempotent on already-absolute/tilde-free paths (expandHome is a no-op for them). No external services to mock; the integration test sets HOME via t.Setenv and SKILLDOZER_CONFIG/SKILLDOZER_SKILLS_DIR via t.Setenv, and t.Chdir(t.TempDir()) to escape the repo walk-up rule.
4. OUTPUT: `skilldozer init --store ~/sub` (with HOME set) writes config store = $HOME/sub and creates that dir; interactive `~/myskills` resolves to $HOME/myskills (not <cwd>/~/myskills). Consumed by: the acceptance suite and the README init section.
5. DOCS: [Mode A] Update the resolveStore doc comment (main.go:865 region) to note ~ is expanded before filepath.Abs (Issue 5). README init section (README.md ~line 62-77) is swept by the final Mode B task (P1.M3.T1) to mention ~ is accepted.

tasks.title: Trim .gitignore to the exact PRD §16 5-entry spec (Issue 6)
tasks.description: The .gitignore carries extra entries (/build, node_modules/, venv/, .env, .pi-subagents/) and section comments beyond the §16 canonical 5-entry set. Rewrite it to exactly the §16 block. This was flagged in a prior round and is still present.

subtasks.title: Rewrite .gitignore to the §16 canonical 5-entry set
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See architecture/bug_fixes_validation.md §ISSUE 6 and architecture/decisions.md §D5. PRD §16 (PRD.md:423-431) spec is EXACTLY these 5 entries with NO comments: `/skilldozer`, `/dist`, `*.test`, `*.out`, `.DS_Store`. The current .gitignore has 5 extra entries (/build, node_modules/, venv/, .env, .pi-subagents/) plus 5 section-comment lines. Verification: `diff <(sed -n '426,430p' PRD.md) .gitignore` must produce no output post-fix.
2. INPUT: none (static file rewrite).
3. LOGIC: Implement PRD §16. Overwrite .gitignore with exactly the 5 canonical lines (no comments, no trailing extras). The 5 kept entries map 1:1 to PRD.md:426-430. Removing .pi-subagents/ makes the agent-artifacts dir untracked (surfaces in git status) — intended per §D5 (do not bless extras); this is a noted residual risk, not a blocker. This is a config/repo-hygiene surface change; treat the file content itself as the test (the diff against §16 is the acceptance check). No external services to mock.
4. OUTPUT: .gitignore byte-for-byte matches the §16 5-entry block. Consumed by: the §16 acceptance gate and git.
5. DOCS: [Mode A] The .gitignore IS the doc/config surface; the §16 canonical block has no comments by design (per architecture/decisions.md §D5). No separate doc edit needed. README does not enumerate .gitignore contents, so no README change.

tasks.title: Document that check/init are reserved tags (Issue 7)
tasks.description: A skill whose canonical tag is literally check or init cannot be resolved by that tag because parseArgs treats check/init as reserved subcommand tokens. The code documents this as deliberate. This is a documentation-only fix: surface the reservation and the workarounds in the README skill-tag section. No code change (a code change would silently shadow the check/init subcommands — a worse UX surprise).

subtasks.title: Add a reserved-tag names note to the README skill-tag section
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See architecture/bug_fixes_validation.md §ISSUE 7 and architecture/decisions.md §D6. parseArgs `case "check"` (main.go:247) and `case "init"` (main.go:268) reserve those tokens BEFORE the default tag-capture branch (main.go:281); the reservation is already commented as deliberate in code. Prior-round §D7 set the 'no code change; documentation-only' precedent. The decision is recorded in architecture/decisions.md §D6. PRD.md is human-owned/READ-ONLY — do NOT edit PRD.md (the §7.2 note the PRD suggests is a human action item recorded in decisions.md, not executed here).
2. INPUT: the README skill-tag section (README.md 'Where skills live' ~line 180+, which defines the canonical tag as the skill dir's path relative to skills/).
3. LOGIC: Add a short 'Reserved tag names' note to the README skill-tag section stating that `check` and `init` are reserved subcommand names (§8.2, §9) and are never treated as skill tags, and listing the workarounds: a skill at skills/check/SKILL.md is still discoverable by --list/--all, by a NESTED path (e.g. writing/check), by its frontmatter name, or by an alias — but cannot be resolved via `skilldozer check` / `skilldozer init`. Frame it as the standard CLI subcommand-reservation rule. Keep it concise and consistent with the existing README voice. No code change; no PRD.md edit. No external services to mock.
4. OUTPUT: README documents the reserved-tag rule and workarounds. Consumed by: users reading the README and by the final Mode B consistency sweep (P1.M3.T1).
5. DOCS: [Mode A] This subtask's entire deliverable IS a doc edit (README skill-tag section). It is the per-feature doc (Mode A) for Issue 7; the final Mode B sweep (P1.M3.T1) verifies README consistency across all 7 fixes but does not duplicate this note.

milestones.title: Changeset-level documentation sync
milestones.description: Mode B sweep: ensure README.md and any overview doc reflect the whole changeset coherently after all implementing subtasks land. The per-subtask Mode A doc edits handle inline comments and the directly-touched sections; this final task verifies README consistency across the stdout-contract change (Issue 1), the new --store exit-2 behavior (Issue 2), tags+--path exclusivity (Issue 3), and tilde support in init (Issue 5).

tasks.title: Sync changeset-level documentation (README + overview)
tasks.description: Sweep README.md for changeset-level coherence: init output contract (stdout = store path only; check report on stderr), the --store-missing-value exit-2 rule, tags+--path mutual exclusion, and ~ expansion in init paths. Verify the reserved-tag note from P1.M2.T5.S1 reads consistently with the error-contract section. This runs LAST and depends on every implementing subtask.

subtasks.title: Sweep README.md init/error-contract sections for the changeset delta
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See architecture/system_context.md (README touchpoints), architecture/bug_fixes_validation.md (all issues), and architecture/decisions.md §D7. README candidates: init section (README.md ~line 62-77 — confirm it states stdout carries the store path and the check report is on stderr, and mention ~ is accepted in init/--store paths); error-contract section (README.md ~line 164-170 — currently lists --path/--list/--search/--all as mutually exclusive and 'combining any two exits 2'; ensure tags+--path is reflected and the --store-missing-value exit-2 rule is mentioned or implied). The reserved-tag note from P1.M2.T5.S1 must read consistently with the error-contract and skill-tag sections.
2. INPUT: the final state of main.go (all fixes landed) and README.md. The per-subtask Mode A edits already updated inline code comments; this task only touches README.md overview/feature sections.
3. LOGIC: Implement SOW §5 Mode B. Read README.md end-to-end and reconcile: (a) init output contract (Issue 1) — if README describes init's stdout, ensure it says ONLY the store path; (b) --store no-value exit 2 (Issue 2) — add to the error-contract section if it enumerates exit-2 cases; (c) tags+--path exclusivity (Issue 3) — the error-contract section lists the mutually-exclusive modes; ensure a tag plus any of them exits 2; (d) ~ in init paths (Issue 5) — mention init/--store accept ~ where appropriate. Do NOT restate per-feature docs that already ride with their subtask (e.g. the reserved-tag note from P1.M2.T5.S1) — only verify consistency. Keep edits minimal and in the existing README voice. Run the repo's doc sanity (re-build the binary, re-run go test ./...) to confirm no code regression from doc-adjacent changes. No external services to mock.
4. OUTPUT: README.md coherently reflects the whole changeset. Consumed by: end users and the §13/§16 acceptance review.
5. DOCS: [Mode B] This IS the changeset-level documentation sync task. It must run after every implementing subtask (its dependencies). If, after review, a README section needs no change, note that explicitly rather than forcing an edit — let the implementing-agent judgment decide whether a touch is warranted.
